classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXIX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have been born into this world, and added to the people of God, at that period wherein already the herb from a grain of mustard seed hath spread out its branches; wherein already the leaven, which at first was contemptible, hath leavened three measures, that is, the whole round world repeopled by the three sons of Noe: for from East and West and North and South shall come they that shall sit down with the Patriarchs, while those shall have been driven without, that have been born of their flesh and have not imitated their faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto his glory then of Christ’s Church our eyes we have opened; and that barren one, for whom joy was proclaimed and foretold, because she was to have more sons than she that had the husband, her we have found to be such an one as hath forgotten the reproaches and infamy of her widowhood: and so we may perhaps wonder when we chance to read in any prophecy the words of Christ’s humiliation, or our own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it may be, that we are less affected by them; because we have not come at that time when these things were read with zest, in that tribulation abounded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But again if we think of the abundance of tribulations, and observe the way wherein we are walking (if indeed we do walk in it), how narrow it is, and how through straits and tribulations it leadeth unto rest everlasting, and how that very thing which in human affairs is called felicity, is more to be feared than misery; since indeed misery ofttimes doth bring out of tribulation a good fruit, but felicity doth corrupt the soul with a perverse security, and giveth place for the Devil the Tempter—when, I say, we shall have judged prudently and rightly, as the salted victim did, that “human life upon earth is trial,” and that no one is at all secure, nor ought to be secure, until he be come to that country, whence no one that is a friend goeth forth, into which no one that is an enemy is admitted, even now in the very glory of the Church we acknowledge the voices of our tribulation: and being members of Christ, subject to our Head in the bond of love, and mutually supporting one another, we will say from the Psalms, that which here we have found the Martyrs said, who were before us; that tribulation is common to all men from the beginning even unto the end.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Title of the Psalm is: “Unto the end, in behalf of those that shall be changed, to David himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now of the change for the better hear thou; for change either is for the worse or for the better.…That we have been changed then for the worse, to ourselves let us ascribe: that for the better we are changed, let us praise God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For those,” then, “that shall be changed,” this Psalm is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whence hath this change been made but by the Passion of Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The very word Pascha in Latin is interpreted passage.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Pascha is not a Greek word but a Hebrew.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It soundeth indeed in the Greek language like Passion, because π€σχειν signifieth to suffer: but if the Hebrew expression be examined, it pointeth to something else.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pascha doth intimate passage.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of which even John the Evangelist hath admonished us, who (just before the Passion when the Lord was coming to the supper wherein He set forth the Sacrament of His Body and Blood) thus speaketh: “But when there had come the hour, wherein Jesus was to pass from this world to the Father.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath expressed then the “passage” of the Pascha.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But unless He passed Himself hence to the Father, who came for our sake, how should we have been able to pass hence, who have not come down for the sake of taking up anything, but have fallen?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He Himself fell not; He but came down, in order that He might raise up him that had fallen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The passage therefore both of Him and of us is hence to the Father, from this world to the kingdom of Heaven, from life mortal to life everlasting, from life earthly to life heavenly, from life corruptible to life incorruptible, from intimacy with tribulations to perpetual security.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Accordingly, “In behalf of them that shall be changed,” the Psalm’s title is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The cause therefore of our change, that is, the very Passion of the Lord and our own voice in tribulations in the text of the Psalm let us observe, let us join in knowing, join in groaning, and in hearing, in joint-knowing, joint-groaning, let us be changed, in order that there may be fulfilled in us the Title of the Psalm, “In behalf of them that shall be changed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Save me, O God, for the waters have entered in even unto my soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That grain is despised now, that seemeth to give forth humble words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the garden it is buried, though the world will admire the greatness of the herb, of which herb the seed was despised by the Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in very deed observe ye the seed of the mustard, minute, dull coloured, altogether despicable, in order that therein may be fulfilled that which hath been said, We have seen Him, and He had neither form nor comeliness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He saith, that waters have come in even unto His soul; because those multitudes, which under the name of waters He hath pointed out, were able so far to prevail as to kill Christ.…Whence then doth He so cry out, as though He were suffering something against His will, except because the Head doth prefigure the Members?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He suffered because He willed: but the Martyrs even though they willed not; for to Peter thus He foretold his passion: “When thou shalt be old,” He saith, “another shall gird thee, and lead thee whither thou wilt not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For though we desire to cleave to Christ, yet we are unwilling to die: and therefore willingly or rather patiently we suffer, because no other passage is given us, through which we may cleave to Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if we could in any other way arrive at Christ, that is, at life everlasting, who would be willing to die?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For while explaining our nature, that is, a sort of association of soul and body, and in these two parts a kind of intimacy of gluing and fastening together, the Apostle saith, that “we have a House not made with hands, everlasting in the Heavens:” that is, immortality prepared for us, wherewith we are to be clothed at the end, when we shall have risen from the dead; and he saith, “Wherein we are not willing to be stripped, but to be clothed upon, that the mortal may be swallowed up of life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If it might so be, we should so will, he saith, to become immortal, as that now that same immortality might come, and now as we are it should change us, in order that this our mortal body by life should be swallowed up, and the body should not be laid aside through death, so as at the end again to have to be recovered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although then from evil to good things we pass, nevertheless the very passage is somewhat bitter, and hath the gall which the Jews gave to the Lord in the Passion, hath something sharp to be endured, whereby they are shown that gave Him vinegar to drink.…For here both sweet are temporal pleasures, and bitter are temporal tribulations: but who would not drink the cup of tribulation temporal, fearing the fire of hell; and who would not contemn the sweetness of the world, longing for the sweetness of life eternal?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From hence that we may be delivered let us cry: lest perchance amidst oppressions we consent to iniquity, and truly irreparably we be swallowed up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fixed I am in the clay of the deep, and there is no substance” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What called the clay?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it those very persons that have persecuted?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For out of clay man hath been made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But these men by falling from righteousness have become the clay of the deep, and whosoever shall not have consented to them persecuting and desiring to draw him to iniquity, out of his clay doth make gold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the clay of the same shall merit to be converted into a heavenly form, and to be made associate of those of whom saith the Title of the Psalm, “in behalf of them that shall be changed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But at the time when these were the clay of the deep, I stuck in them: that is, they held Me, prevailed against Me, killed Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fixed” then “I am in the clay of the deep, and there is no substance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this, “there is no substance”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can it be that clay itself is not a substance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is then, “fixed I am”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can it be that Christ hath thus stuck?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or hath He stuck, and was not, as hath been said in the book of Job, “the earth delivered into the hands of the ungodly man”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was He fixed in body, because it could be held, and suffered even crucifixion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For unless with nails He had been fixed, crucified He had not been.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence then “there is no substance”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is that clay not a substance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we shall understand, if it be possible, what is, “and there is no substance,” if first we shall have understood what is a substance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is substance spoken of even of riches, as we say, he hath substance, and he hath lost substance.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God is a sort of substance: for that which is no substance, is nothing at all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To be a substance then is to be something.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence also in the Catholic Faith against the poisons of certain heretics thus we are builded up, so that we say, Father and Son and Holy Spirit are of one substance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, of one substance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For example, if gold is the Father, gold is also the Son, gold also the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever the Father is because He is God, the same is the Son, the same the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when He is the Father, this is not what He is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Father He is called not in reference to Himself, but in reference to the Son: but in reference to Himself God He is called.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore in that He is God, by the same He is a substance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because of the same substance the Son is, without doubt the Son also is God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But yet in that He is Father, because it is not the name of the substance, but is referred to the Son; we do not say that the Son is Father in the same manner as we say the Son is God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou askest what the Father is; we answer, God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou askest what is the Father and the Son: we answer, God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If questioned of the Father alone, answer thou God: if questioned of both, not Gods, but God, answer thou.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We do not reply as in the case of men, when thou inquirest what is father Abraham, we answer a man; the substance of him serveth for answer: thou inquirest what is his son Isaac, we answer, a man; of the same substance are Abraham and Isaac: thou inquirest what is Abraham and Isaac, we answer not man, but men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not so in things divine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so great in this case is the fellowship of substance, that of equality it alloweth, plurality alloweth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then it shall have been said to thee, when thou tellest me that the Son is the same as the Father, in fact the Son also is the Father; answer thou, according to the substance I have told thee that the Son is the same as the Father, not according to that term which is used in reference to something else.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in reference to Himself He is called God, in reference to the Father is called Son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And again, the Father in reference to Himself is called God, in reference to the Son He is called Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Father as He is called in reference to the Son, is not the Son: the Son as He is called in reference to the Father, is not the Father: what the Father is called in reference to Himself and the Son in reference to Himself, the same is Father and Son, that is, God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is then, “there is no substance”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After this interpretation of substance, how shall we be able to understand this passage of the Psalm, “Fixed I am in the clay of the deep, and there is no substance”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God made man, He made substance; and O that he had continued in that which God made Him!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If man had continued in that which God made him, in him would not have been fixed He whom God begot.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But moreover because through iniquity man fell from the substance wherein he was made (for iniquity itself is no substance; for iniquity is not a nature which God formed, but a perverseness which man made); the Son of God came to the clay of the deep, and was fixed; and that was no substance wherein He was fixed, because in the iniquity of them He was fixed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All things by Him were made, and without Him there was made nothing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All natures by Him were made, iniquity by Him was not made, because iniquity was not made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those substances by Him were made, which praise Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The whole creation praising God is commemorated by the three children in the furnace, and from things earthly to things heavenly, or from things heavenly to things earthly reacheth the hymn of them praising God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not that all these things have sense to praise; but because all things being well meditated upon, do beget praise, and the heart by considering creation is fulfilled to overflowing with a hymn to the Creator.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All things do praise God, but only the things which God hath made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do ye observe in that hymn that covetousness praiseth God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There even the serpent praiseth God, covetousness praiseth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all creeping things are there named in the praise of God: there are named all creeping things; but there are not there named any vices.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For vices out of ourselves and out of our own will we have: and vices are not a substance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In these was fixed the Lord, when He suffered persecution: in the vice of the Jews, not in the substance of men which by Him was made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have come into the depth of the sea, and the tempest hath made Me to sink down.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thanks to the mercy of Him who came into the depth of the sea, and vouchsafed to be swallowed by the sea whale, but was vomited forth the third day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came into the depth of the sea, in which depth we were thrust down, in which depth we had suffered shipwreck: He came thither Himself, and the tempest made Him to sink down: for there He suffered waves, those very men; tempests, the voices of men saying, “Crucify, Crucify.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though Pilate said, I find not any cause in this Man why He should be killed: there prevailed the voices of them, saying, “Crucify, Crucify.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The tempest increased, until He was made to sink down that had come into the depth of the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the Lord suffered in the hands of the Jews that which He suffered not when upon the waters He was walking: the which not only He had not suffered Himself, but had not allowed even Peter to suffer it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have laboured, crying, hoarse have become my jaws” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where was this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When was this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us question the Gospel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Passion of our Lord in this Psalm we perceive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, indeed, that He suffered we know; that there came in waters even unto His Soul, because peoples prevailed even unto His death, we read, we believe; in the tempest that He was sunk down, because tumult prevailed to His killing, we acknowledge: but that He laboured in crying, and that His jaws were made hoarse, not only we read not, but even on the contrary we read, that He answered not to them a word, in order that there might be fulfilled that which in another Psalm hath been said, “I have become as it were a man not hearing, and having not in his mouth reproofs.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that which in Isaiah hath been prophesied, “like a sheep to be sacrificed He was led, and like a lamb before one shearing Him, so He opened not His mouth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He became like a man not hearing, and having not in His mouth reproofs, how did He labour crying, and how were His jaws made hoarse?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it that He was even then silent, because He was hoarse with having cried so much in vain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this indeed we know to have been His voice on the Cross out of a certain Psalm: “O God, My God, why hast Thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how great was that voice, or of how long duration, that in it His jaws should have become hoarse?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Long while He cried, “Woe unto you, Scribes and Pharisees:” long while He cried, “Woe unto the world because of offences.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And truly hoarse in a manner He cried, and therefore was not understood, when the Jews said, What is this that He saith?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hard is this saying, who is able to hear it?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know not what He saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said all these words: but hoarse were His jaws to them that understood not His words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine eyes have failed from hoping in My God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far be it that this should be taken of the person of the Head: far be it that His eyes should have failed from hoping in His God: in whom rather there was God reconciling the world to Himself, and Who was the Word made flesh and dwelled in us, so that not only God was in Him, but also He was Himself God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not so then: the eyes of Himself, our Head, failed not from hoping in His God: but the eyes of Him have failed in His Body, that is, in His members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This voice is of the members, this voice is of the Body, not of the Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then do we find it in His Body and members?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus “there have been multiplied above the hairs of My head they that hate Me gratis” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How multiplied?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So as that they might add to themselves even one out of the twelve.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There have been multiplied above the hairs of My head they that hate Me for nought.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With the hairs of His head He hath compared His enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason they were shorn when in the place of Calvary He was crucified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the members accept this voice, let them learn to be hated gratis.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now, O Christian, if it must needs be that the world hate thee, why dost thou not make it hate thee gratis, in order that in the Body of thy Lord and in this Psalm sent before concerning Him, thou mayest acknowledge thy own voice?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How shall it come to pass that the world hate thee gratis?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou no wise hurtest any one, and art still hated: for this is gratis, without cause…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, Thou hast known mine improvidence” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again out of the mouth of the Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what improvidence is there in Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is He not Himself the Virtue of God, and the Wisdom of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth He call this His improvidence, whereof the Apostle speaketh, “the foolishness of God is wiser than men”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Mine improvidence, that very thing which in Me they derided that seem to themselves to be wise, Thou hast known why it was done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what was so much like improvidence, as, when He had it in His power with one word to lay low the persecutors, to suffer Himself to be held, scourged, spit upon, buffeted, with thorns to be crowned, to the tree to be nailed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is like improvidence, it seemeth a foolish thing; but this foolish thing excelleth all wise men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Foolish indeed it is: but even when grain falleth into the earth, if no one knoweth the custom of husbandmen, it seemeth foolish…Improvidence it appeareth; but hope maketh it not to be improvidence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then spared not Himself: because even the Father spared Him not, but delivered Him up for us all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And of the Same, “Who loved me,” saith the Apostle, “and delivered up Himself for me:” for except a grain shall have fallen into the land so that it die, fruit, He saith, it will not yield.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the improvidence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my transgressions from Thee are not concealed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is plain, clear, open, that this must be perceived to be out of the mouth of the Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Transgressions none had Christ: He was the bearer of transgressions, but not the committer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Are not concealed:” that is, I have confessed to Thee, all my transgressions, and before my mouth Thou hast seen them in my thought, hast seen the wounds which Thou wast to heal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even in the Body, in the members: in those believers out of whom there was now cleaving to Him that member, who was confessing his sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them not blush in Me, that wait for Thee, O Lord, Lord of virtues” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, the voice of the Head, “Let them not blush in Me:” let it not be said to them, Where is He on whom ye were relying?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let it not be said to them, Where is He that was saying to you, Believe ye in God, and in Me believe?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them not blush in Me, that wait for Thee,” O Lord, Lord of virtues.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them not be confounded concerning Me, that seek Thee, O God of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This also may be understood of the Body, but only if thou consider the Body of Him not one man: for in truth one man is not the Body of Him, but a small member, but the Body is made up of members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the full Body of Him is the whole Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason then saith the Church, “Let them not blush in Me, that wait for Thee, O Lord, Lord of virtues.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For because of Thee I have sustained upbraiding, shamelessness hath covered my face” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No great thing is that which is spoken of in “I have sustained:” but that which is spoken of in “for Thy sake I have sustained,” is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou sustainest because thou hast sinned; for thine own sake thou sustainest, not for the sake of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to you what glory is there, saith Peter, if sinning ye are punished, and ye bear it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if thou sustainest because thou hast kept the commandment of God, truly for the sake of God thou sustainest; and thy reward remaineth for everlasting, because for the sake of God thou hast sustained revilings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to this end He first sustained in order that we might learn to sustain…“Shamelessness hath covered my face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shamelessness is what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not to be confused.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, it seemeth to be as it were a fault, when we say, the man is shameless.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great is the shamelessness of the man, that he doth not blush.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore shamelessness is a kind of folly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A Christian ought to have this shamelessness, when he cometh among men to whom Christ is an offence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he shall have blushed because of Christ, he will be blotted out from the book of the living.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou must needs therefore have shamelessness when Thou art reviled because of Christ; when they say, Worshipper of the Crucified, adorer of Him that died ill, venerator of Him that was slain! here if thou shalt blush thou art a dead man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For see the sentence of Him that deceiveth no one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He that shall have been ashamed of Me before men, I will also be ashamed of him before the Angels of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Watch therefore thyself whether there be in thee shamelessness; be thou boldfaced, when thou hearest a reproach concerning Christ; yea be boldfaced.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why fearest thou for thy forehead which thou hast armed with the sign of the Cross?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “An alien I have become to My brethren, and a stranger to the sons of My mother” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the sons of the Synagogue He became a stranger…Why so?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why did they not acknowledge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why did they call Him an alien?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why did they dare to say, we know not whence He is?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because the zeal of Thine House hath eaten Me up:” that is, because I have persecuted in them their own iniquities, because I have not patiently borne those whom I have rebuked, because I have sought Thy glory in Thy House, because I have scourged them that in the Temple dealt unseemly: in which place also there is quoted, “the zeal of Thine House hath eaten Me up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence an alien, hence a Stranger; hence, we know not whence He is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They would have acknowledged whence I am, if they had acknowledged that which Thou hast commanded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if I had found them keeping Thy commandments, the zeal of Thine House would not have eaten Me up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the reproaches of men reproaching Thee have fallen upon Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this testimony Paul the Apostle hath also made use (there hath been read but now the very lesson), and saith, “Whatsoever things aforetime have been written, have been written that we might be instructed.”…Why “Thee”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is the Father reproached, and not Christ Himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why have “the reproaches of men reproaching Thee fallen upon Me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because, “he that hath known Me, hath known the Father also:” because no one hath reviled Christ without reviling God: because no one honoureth the Father, except he that honoureth the Son also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I have covered in fasting My Soul, and it became to Me for a reviling” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His fasting was, when there fell away all they that had believed in Him; because also it was His hunger, that men should believe in Him: because also it was His thirst, when He said to the woman, I thirst, “give Me to drink:” yea for her faith He was thirsting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And from the Cross when He was saying, “I thirst,” He was seeking the faith of them for whom He had said, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what did those men give to drink to Him thirsty?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Vinegar is also called old.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason of the old man they gave to drink, because they willed not to be new.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why willed they not to become new?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because to the title of this Psalm whereon is written, “For them that shall be changed,” they belonged not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “I have covered in fasting My Soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, He put from Him even the gall which they offered: He chose rather to fast than to accept bitterness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they enter not into His Body that are embittered, whereof in another place a Psalm saith, “They that are embittered shall not be exalted in themselves.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “I have covered in fasting My Soul: and it became to Me for a reviling.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This very thing became to Me for a reviling, that I consented not to them, that is, from them I fasted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he that consenteth not to men seducing to evil, fasteth from them; and through this fasting earneth reviling, so that he is upbraided because he consenteth not to the evil thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I have set sackcloth my garment” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already before we have said something of the sackcloth, from whence there is this, “But I, when they were troubling Me, was covering myself with sackcloth, and was humbling My Soul in fasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have set sackcloth for My garment:” that is, have set against them My flesh, on which to spend their rage, I have concealed My divinity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sackcloth,” because mortal the flesh was: in order that by sin He might condemn sin in the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I have set sackcloth my garment: and I have been made to them for a parable,” that is, for a derision.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is called a parable, whenever a comparison is made concerning some one, when he is evil spoken of.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “So may this man perish,” for example, “as that man did,” is a parable: that is, a comparison and likeness in cursing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have been made to them,” then, “for a parable.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Against Me were reviling they that were sitting in the gate” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the gate” is nothing else but in public.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And against Me they were chanting, they that were drinking wine.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do ye think, brethren, that this hath befallen Christ alone?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Daily to Him in His members it happeneth: whenever perchance it is necessary for the servant of God to forbid excess of wine and luxuries in any village or town, where there hath not been heard the Word of God, it is not enough that they sing, nay more even against him they begin to sing, by whom they are forbidden to sing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Compare ye now His fasting and their wine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I with My prayer with Thee, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I was with Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With Thee by praying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when thou art evil spoken of, and knowest not what thou mayest do; when at thee are hurled reproaches, and thou findest not any way of rebuking him by whom they are hurled; nothing remaineth for thee but to pray.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But remember even for that very man to pray.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I with my prayer with Thee, O Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is the time of Thy good pleasure, O God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For behold the grain is being buried, there shall spring up fruit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It is the time of Thy good pleasure, O God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this time even the Prophets have spoken, whereof the Apostle maketh mention: “Behold now the time acceptable, behold now the day of salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It is the time of Thy good pleasure, O God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the multitude of Thy mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the time of good pleasure, “in the multitude of Thy mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if there were not a multitude of Thy mercy, what should we do for the multitude of our iniquity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the multitude of Thy mercy; Hearken to me in the truth of Thy Salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He hath said, “of Thy mercy,” he hath added truth also: for “mercy and truth” are all the ways of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In forgiving sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In fulfilling the promises.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Save Thou Me from the mire, that I may not stick” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From that whereof above he had spoken, “Fixed I am in the clay of the deep, and there is no substance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Furthermore, since ye have duly received the exposition of that expression, in this place there is nothing further for you to hear particularly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From hence he saith that he must be delivered, wherein before he said that he was fixed: “Save Thou Me from the mire, that I may not stick.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he explaineth this himself: “Let Me be rescued from them that hate Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were themselves therefore the clay wherein he had stuck.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the following perchance suggesteth itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A little before he had said, Fixed I am; now he saith, Save Thou Me from the mire, that I may not stick:” whereas after the meaning of what was said before he ought to have said, Save Thou Me from the mire where I had stuck, by rescuing Me, not by causing that I stick not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore He had stuck in flesh, but had not stuck in spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith this, because of the infirmity of His members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whenever perchance thou art seized by one that urgeth thee to iniquity, thy body indeed is taken, in regard to the body thou art fixed in the clay of the deep: but so long as thou consentest not, thou hast not stuck; but if thou consentest, thou hast stuck.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let then thy prayer be in that place, in order that as thy body is now held, so thy soul may not be held, so thou mayest be free in bonds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let not the tempest of waters drown Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But already he had been drowned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have come into the depth of the sea,” thou hast said, and “the tempest hath drowned Me,” thou hast said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath drowned after the flesh, let it not drown after the Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They to whom was said, If they shall have persecuted you in one city, flee ye into another; had this said to them, that neither in flesh they should stick, nor in spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we must not desire to stick even in flesh; but as far as we are able we ought to avoid it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if we shall have stuck, and shall have fallen into the hands of sinners: then in body we have stuck, we are fixed in the clay of the deep, it remaineth to entreat for the soul that we stick not, that is, that we consent not, that the tempest of water drown us not, so that we go into the deep of the clay.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Neither let the deep swallow Me, nor the pit close her mouth upon Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath he prayed against?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great is the pit of the depth of human iniquity: every one, if he shall have fallen into it, will fall into the deep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But yet if a man being there placed confesseth his sins to his God, the pit will not shut her mouth upon him: as is written in another Psalm, “From the depths I have cried to Thee, O Lord; Lord, hearken unto my voice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if there is done in him that which another passage of Scripture saith, “When a sinner shall have come into the depth of evil things, he will despise,” upon him the pit hath shut her mouth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why hath she shut her mouth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because she hath shut his mouth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath lost confession, really dead he is, and there is fulfilled in him that which elsewhere is spoken of, “From a dead man, as from one that is not, there perisheth confession.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hearken unto me, O Lord, for sweet is Thy mercy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath given this as a reason why He ought to be hearkened unto, because sweet is the mercy of God.…To a man set in trouble the mercy of God must needs be sweet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Concerning this sweetness of the mercy of God see ye what in another place the Scripture saith: “Like rain in drought, so beautiful is the mercy of God in trouble.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which there he saith to be “beautiful,” the same he saith here to be “sweet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not even bread would be sweet, unless hunger had preceded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore even when the Lord permitteth or causeth us to be in any trouble, even then He is merciful: for He doth not withdraw nourishment, but stirreth up longing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Accordingly what saith he now, “Hearken to me, O Lord, for sweet is Thy mercy”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now do not Thou defer hearkening, in so great trouble I am, that sweet to me is Thy mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to this end Thou didst defer to succour, in order that to me that wherewith Thou didst succour might be sweet: but now no longer is there cause why Thou must defer; my trouble hath arrived at the appointed measure of distress, let Thy mercy come to do the work of goodness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “After the multitude of Thy pities have regard unto me:” not after the multitude of my sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn not away Thy face from Thy child” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this is a commending of humility; “from Thy child,” that is, “from Thy little one:” because now I have been rid of pride through the discipline of tribulation, “turn not away Thy face from Thy child.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is that beautiful mercy of God, whereof he spake above.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the following verse he explaineth that whereof he spake: “For I am troubled, speedily hearken Thou unto me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “speedily”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now there is no cause why Thou must defer it: I am troubled, my affliction hath gone before; let Thy mercy follow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Give heed to my soul, and redeem her,” doth need no exposition: let us see therefore what followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because of mine enemies deliver me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This petition is evidently wonderful, neither briefly to be touched upon, nor hastily to be skipped over; truly wonderful: “Because of mine enemies deliver me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Because of mine enemies deliver me”?…I see no reason for this petition, “Because of mine enemies deliver me:” unless we understand it of something else, which when I shall have spoken by the help of the Lord, He shall judge in you, that dwelleth in you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a kind of secret deliverance of holy men: this for their own sakes is made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is one public and evident: this is made because of their enemies, either for their punishment, or for their deliverance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For truly God delivered not the brothers in the book of Maccabees from the fires of the persecutor.…But again the Three Children openly were delivered from the furnace of fire; because their body also was rescued, their safety was public.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The former were in secret crowned, the latter openly delivered: all however saved.…There is then a secret deliverance, there is an open deliverance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Secret deliverance doth belong to the soul, open deliverance to the body as well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in secret the soul is delivered, openly the body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, if so it be, in this Psalm the voice of the Lord let us acknowledge: to the secret deliverance doth belong that whereof he spake above, “Give heed to my soul, and redeem her.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There remaineth the body’s deliverance: for on His arising and ascending into the Heavens, and sending the Holy Ghost from above, there were converted to His faith they that at His death did rage, and out of enemies they were made friends through His grace, not through their righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore he hath continued, “Because of mine enemies deliver me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Give heed to my soul,” but this in secret: but “because of mine enemies deliver” even my body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For mine enemies it will profit nothing if soul alone Thou shalt have delivered; that they have done something, that they have accomplished something, they will believe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “What profit is there in my blood, while I go down into corruption?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “give heed to my soul, and redeem her,” which Thou alone knowest: secondly also, “because of mine enemies deliver me,” that my flesh may not see corruption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou knowest my reproach, and my confusion, and my shame” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is reproach?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is confusion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Reproach is that which the enemy casteth in the teeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Confusion is that which gnaweth the conscience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shame is that which causeth even a noble brow to blush, because of the upbraiding with a pretended crime.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is no crime; or even if there is a crime, it doth not belong to him, against whom it is alleged: but yet the infirmity of the human mind ofttimes is made ashamed even when a pretended crime is alleged; not because it is alleged, but because it is believed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these things are in the Body of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For confusion in Him could not be, in whom guilt was not found.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was alleged as a crime against Christians, the very fact that they were Christians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That indeed was glory: the brave gladly received it, and so received it as that they blushed not at all for the Lord’s name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For fearlessness had covered the face of them, having the effrontery of Paul, saying, “for I blush not because of the Gospel: for the virtue of God it is for salvation to every one believing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Paul, art not thou a venerator of the Crucified?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Little it is, he saith, for me not to blush for it: nay, therein alone I glory, wherefore the enemy thinketh me to blush.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But from me far be it to glory, save in the Cross of Jesus Christ, through whom to me the world is crucified, and I to the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At such a brow as this then reproach alone could be hurled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For neither could there be confusion in a conscience already made whole, nor shame in a brow so free.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when it was being alleged against certain that they had slain Christ, deservedly they were pricked through with evil conscience, and to their health confounded and converted, so that they could say, “Thou hast known my confusion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou therefore, O Lord, hast known not only my reproach but also my confusion, in certain shame also: who, though in me they believe, publicly blush to confess me before ungodly men, human tongue having more influence with them than promise divine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold ye therefore them: even such are commended to God, not that so He may leave them, but that by aiding them He may make them perfect.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a certain man believing and wavering hath said, “I believe, O Lord, help Thou mine unbelief.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thy sight are all they that trouble Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why I have reproach, Thou knowest; why confusion, Thou knowest; why shame, Thou knowest: therefore deliver Thou me because of mine enemies, because Thou knowest these things of me, they know not; and thus, because they are themselves in Thy sight, not knowing these things, they will not be able to be either confounded or corrected, unless openly Thou shalt have delivered me because of mine enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Reproach my heart hath expected, and misery.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “hath expected”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath foreseen these things as going to be, hath foretold them as going to be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He came not for any other purpose.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He had been unwilling to die, neither would He have willed to be born: for the sake of resurrection He did both.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there were two particular things known to us among mankind, but one thing unknown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we knew that men were born and died: that they rose again and lived for everlasting we knew not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That He might show to us that which we knew not, He took upon Him the two things which we knew.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this end therefore He came.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Reproach my heart hath expected and misery.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the misery of whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He expected misery, but rather of the crucifiers, rather of the persecutors, that in them should be misery, in Him mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For pitying the misery of them even while hanging on the Cross, He saith, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then did it profit, that I expected?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, what did it profit that I foretold?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What did it profit that I said to this end I had come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I came to fulfil that which I said, “I waited for one that together should be made sorrowful, and there was not; and men comforting, and I found not:” that is, there was none.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that which in the former verse He said, “I waited for one that together should be made sorrowful,” the same is in the following verse, “and men comforting.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that which in the former verse is, “and there was not;” the same in the following verse is, “and I found not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore another sentence is not added, but the former is repeated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which sentence if we reconsider, a question may arise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For were His disciples nowise made sorrowful when He was led to the Passion, when on the tree hanged, when dead?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So much were they made sorrowful, that Mary Magdalene, who first saw Him, rejoicing told them as they were mourning what she had seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Gospel speaketh of these things: it is not our presumption, not our suspicion: it is evident that the disciples grieved, it is evident that they mourned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Strange women were weeping, when to the Passion He was being led, unto whom turning He saith, “Weep ye, but for yourselves, do not for Me.”… Peter certainly loved very much, and without hesitation threw himself to walk on the waves, and at the voice of the Lord he was delivered: and though following Him when led to the Passion, with the boldness of love, yet being troubled, thrice he denied Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence, except because an evil thing it seemed to him to die?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he was shunning that which he thought an evil thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then even in the Lord he was lamenting, which he was himself shunning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On this account even before he had said, “Far be it from Thee, O Lord, merciful be Thou to Thyself: there shall not come to pass this thing:” at which time he merited to hear, “Satan;” after that he had heard, “Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore in that sorrowfulness which the Lord felt because of those for whom He prayed, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do:” no companion He found.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I waited for one that together should be made sorrowful, and there was not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was not at all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And men comforting, and I found not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are men comforting?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men profiting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they comfort us, they are the comfort of all preachers of the Truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And they gave for My food gall, and in My thirst they gave Me vinegar to drink” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was done indeed to the letter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the Gospel declareth this to us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we must understand, brethren, that the very fact that I found not comforters, that the very fact that I found not one that together should be made sorrowful, this was My gall, this to Me was bitter, this was vinegar: bitter because of grief, vinegar because of their old man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we read, that to Him indeed gall was offered, as the Gospel speaketh; but for drink, not for food.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, we must so take and consider that when fulfilled, which here had been before predicted, “They gave for My food gall:” and in that very action, not only in this saying, we ought to seek for a mystery, at secret things to knock, to enter the rent veil of the Temple, to see there a Sacrament, both in what there hath been said and in what there hath been done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They gave,” He saith, “for My food gall:” not the thing itself which they gave was food, for it was drink: but “for food they gave it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because already the Lord had taken food, and into it there had been thrown gall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He had taken Himself pleasant food, when He ate the Passover with His disciples: therein He showed the Sacrament of His Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto this food so pleasant, so sweet, of the Unity of Christ, of which the Apostle maketh mention, saying, “For one bread, One Body, being many we are;” unto this pleasant food who is there that addeth gall, except the gainsayers of the Gospel, like those persecutors of Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For less the Jews sinned in crucifying Him walking on earth, than they that despise Him sitting in Heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which then the Jews did, in giving above the food which He had already taken that bitter draught to drink, the same they do that by evil living bring scandal upon the Church: the same do embittered heretics, “But let them not be exalted in their own selves.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They give gall after so delectable meat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what doth the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He admitteth them not to His Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this mystery, when they presented gall, the Lord Himself tasted, and would not drink.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we did not suffer them, neither at all should we taste: but because it is necessary to suffer them, we must needs taste.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because in the members of Christ such sort cannot be, they can be tasted, received into the Body they cannot be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And they gave for My food gall, and in My thirst they gave Me vinegar to drink.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I was thirsting, and vinegar I received: that is, for the faith of them I longed, and I found oldness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the table of them be made in their own presence for a trap” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Like the trap which for Me they set, in giving Me such a draught, let such a trap be for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then, “in their own presence”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the table of them be made for a trap,” would have been sufficient.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are such as know their iniquity, and in it most obstinately do persevere: in their own presence there is made a trap for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are they that, being too destructive, “go down into Hell alive.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, of persecutors what hath been said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Except that the Lord were in us, perchance alive they had swallowed us up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is alive?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consenting to them, and knowing that we ought not to consent to them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore in their own presence there is made a trap, and they are not amended.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even though in their own presence there is a trap, let them not fall into it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold they know the trap, and thrust out foot, and bow their necks to be caught.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much better were it to turn away from the trap, to acknowledge sin, to condemn error, to be rid of bitterness, to pass over into the Body of Christ, to seek the Lord’s glory!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But so much prevaileth presumption of mind, that even in their own presence the trap is, and they fall into it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the eyes of them be darkened, that they see not,” followeth here: that whereas without benefit they have seen, it may chance to them even not to see.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the table of them,” therefore, “be made in their own presence for a trap.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not from one wishing, but from one prophesying: not in order that it may come to pass, but because it will come to pass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This we have often remarked, and ye ought to remember it: lest that which the prescient mind saith in the Spirit of God, it should seem with ill will to imprecate.…Let it then be done to them, “both for a requital and for a stumbling-block.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And is this by any means unjust?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is “for a requital.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not anything would happen to them, which was not owed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For a requital” it is done, “and for a stumbling-block:” for they are themselves a stumbling-block to themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the eyes of them be darkened, that they see not, and the back of them alway bow Thou down” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a consequence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they, whose eyes have been darkened that they see not, it followeth, must have their back bowed down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because when they have ceased to take knowledge of things above, they must needs think of things below.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that well heareth, “lift up the heart,” a bowed back hath not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For with stature erect he looketh for the hope laid up for him in Heaven; most especially if he send before him his treasure, whither his heart followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, on the other hand, they perceive not the hope of future life; already being blinded, they think of things below: and this is to have a bowed back: from which disorder the Lord delivered that woman.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Satan hath bound her eighteen years, and her that was bowed down He raised up: and because on the Sabbath He did it, the Jews were scandalized; suitably were they scandalized at her being raised up, themselves being bowed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Pour forth upon them Thine anger, and let the indignation of Thine anger overtake them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 25), are plain words: but nevertheless, in “overtake them” we perceive them as it were fleeing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whither are they to flee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Into Heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art present.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their wings they will not take to fly straight: “Let the indignation of Thine anger overtake them,” let it not permit them to escape.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the habitation of them become forsaken” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is now evident.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the same manner as He hath mentioned not only a secret deliverance of His, saying, “Give heed to My soul, and redeem her;” but also one open after the body, adding, “because of mine enemies deliver me:” so also to these men He foretelleth how there are to be certain secret misfortunes, whereof a little before He was speaking.…For the blindness of the Jews was secret vengeance: but the open was what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let their habitation become forsaken, and in their tabernacles let there not be any one to inhabit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There hath come to pass this thing in the very city Jerusalem, wherein they thought themselves mighty in crying against the Son of God, “Crucify, Crucify;” and in prevailing because they were able to kill Him that raised dead men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How mighty to themselves, how great, they seemed!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There followed afterwards the vengeance of the Lord, stormed was the city, utterly conquered the Jews, slain were I know not how many thousands of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one of the Jews is permitted to come thither now: where they were able to cry against the Lord, there by the Lord they are not permitted to dwell.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have lost the place of their fury: and O that even now they would know the place of their rest!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What profit to them was Caiaphas in saying, “If we shall have let go this man thus, there will come the Romans, and take away from us both place and kingdom”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, both they did not let Him go alive, and He liveth: and there have come the Romans, and have taken from them both place and kingdom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now we heard, when the Gospel was being read, “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, how often would I have gathered together thy sons, as a hen her chickens under her wings, and thou wouldest not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold there is left to you your house forsaken.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Him whom Thou hast smitten they have themselves persecuted, and upon the pain of my wounds they have added” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then have they sinned if they have persecuted one by God smitten?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sin is ascribed to their mind?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the thing was done in Christ which was to be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To suffer indeed He had come, and He punished him through whom He suffered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Judas the traitor was punished, and Christ was crucified: but us He redeemed by His blood, and He punished him in the matter of his price.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he threw down the price of silver, for which by him the Lord had been sold; and he knew not the price wherewith he had himself by the Lord been redeemed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This thing was done in the case of Judas.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when we see that there is a sort of measure of requital in all men, and that not any one can be suffered to rage more than he hath received power to do: how have they “added,” or what is that smiting of the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Without doubt He is speaking in the person of him from whom He had received a body, from whom He had taken unto Him flesh, that is in the person of mankind, of Adam himself who was smitten with the first death because of his sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Mortal therefore here are men born, as born with their punishment: to this punishment they add, whosoever do persecute men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now here man would not have had to die, unless God had smitten him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then dost thou, O man, rage more than this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it little for a man that some time he is to die?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Each one of us therefore beareth his punishment: to this punishment they would add that persecute us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This punishment is the smiting of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord smote man with the sentence: “What day ye shall have touched it,” He saith, “with death ye shall die.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Out of this death He had taken upon Him flesh, and our old man hath been crucified together with Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the voice of that man He hath said these words, “Him whom Thou hast smitten they have themselves persecuted, and upon the pain of My wounds they have added.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Upon what pain of wounds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Upon the pain of sins they have themselves added.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For sins He hath called His wounds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But do not look to the Head, consider the Body; according to the voice whereof hath been said by the Same in that Psalm, wherein He showed there was His voice, because in the first verse thereof He cried from the Cross, “God, My God, look upon Me, why hast Thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There in continuation He saith, “Afar from My safety are the words of Mine offences.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lay Thou iniquity upon their iniquity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who would not be afraid?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To God is said, “Lay Thou iniquity upon their iniquity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence shall God lay iniquity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For hath He iniquity to lay?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we know that to be true which hath been spoken through Paul the Apostle, “What then shall we say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is there anywise iniquity with God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence then, “Lay Thou iniquity upon iniquity”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How must we understand this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May the Lord be with us, that we may speak, and because of your weariness may be able to speak briefly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
