verses.text: Their iniquity was that they killed a just Man: there was added another, that they crucified the Son of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their raging was as though against a man: but “if they had known, the Lord of Glory they had never crucified.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They with their own iniquity willed to kill as it were a man: there was laid iniquity upon their own iniquity, so that the Son of God they should crucify.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who laid this iniquity upon them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that said, “Perchance they will reverence My Son,” Him I will send.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they were wont to kill servants sent to them, to demand rent and profit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He sent the Son Himself, in order that Him also they might kill.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He laid iniquity upon their own iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And these things did God do in wrath, or rather in just requital?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “May it be done to them,” He saith, “for a requital and for a stumbling-block.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They had deserved to be so blinded as not to know the Son of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this God did, laying iniquity upon their iniquity; not in wounding, but in not making whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in like manner as thou increasest a fever, increasest a disorder, not by adding disorder, but by not relieving: so because they were of such sort as that they merited not to be healed, in their very naughtiness in a manner they advanced; as it is said, “But evil men and wicked doers advance for the worse:” and iniquity is laid upon their own iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And let them not enter in Thy righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a plain thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be blotted out from the book of the living” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For had they been some time written therein?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, we must not so take it, as that God writeth any one in the book of life, and blotteth him out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If a man said, “What I have written I have written,” concerning the title where it had been written, “King of the Jews:” doth God write any one, and blot him out?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He foreknoweth, He hath predestined all before the foundation of the world that are to reign with His Son in life everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These He hath written down, these same the Book of Life doth contain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, in the Apocalypse, what saith the Spirit of God, when the same Scripture was speaking of the oppressions that should be from Antichrist?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There shall give consent to him all they that have not been written in the book of life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then without doubt they will not consent that have been written.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then are these men blotted out from that book wherein they were never written?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This hath been said according to their own hope, because they thought of themselves that they were written.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “let them be blotted out from the book of life”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even to themselves let it be evident, that they were not there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By this method of speaking hath been said in another Psalm, “There shall fall from Thy side a thousand, and tens of thousands from on Thy right hand:” that is, many men shall be offended, even out of that number who thought that they would sit with Thee, even out of that number who thought that they would stand at Thy right hand, being severed from the left-hand goats: not that when any one hath there stood, he shall afterwards fall, or when any one with Him hath sat, he shall be cast away; but that many men were to fall into scandal, who already thought themselves to be there, that is, many that thought that they would sit with Thee, many that hoped that they would stand at the right hand, will themselves fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then here also they that hoped as though by the merit of their own righteousness themselves to have been written in the book of God, they to whom is said, “Search the Scriptures, wherein ye think yourselves to have life eternal:” when their condemnation shall have been brought even to their own knowledge, shall be effaced from the book of the living, they shall know themselves not to be there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the verse which followeth, explaineth what hath been said: “And with just men let them not be written.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have said then “Let them be effaced,” according to their hope: but according to Thy justice I say what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Poor and sorrowful I am” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it that we may acknowledge that through bitterness of soul this poor One doth speak evil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He hath spoken of many things to happen to them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as if we were saying to Him, “Why such things?”—“Nay, not so much!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He answereth, “poor and sorrowful I am.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have brought Me to want, unto this sorrow they have set Me down, therefore I say these words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not, however, the indignation of one cursing, but the prediction of one prophesying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He was intending to recommend to us certain things which hereafter He saith of His poverty and His sorrow, in order that we may learn to be poor and sorrowful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “Blessed are the poor, for theirs is the kingdom of Heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, “Blessed are they that mourn, for they shall be comforted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This therefore He doth Himself before now show to us: and so, “poor and sorrowful I am.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The whole Body of Him saith this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Body of Christ in this earth is poor and sorrowful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let Christians be rich.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly if Christians they are, they are poor; in comparison with the riches celestial for which they hope, all their gold they count for sand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the health of Thy countenance, O God, hath taken Me up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is this poor One anywise forsaken?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When dost thou deign to bring near to thy table a poor man in rags?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But again, this poor One the health of the countenance of God hath taken up: in His countenance He hath hidden His need.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of Him hath been said, “Thou shalt hide them in the hiding place of Thy countenance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in that countenance what riches there are would ye know?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Riches here give thee this advantage, that thou mayest dine on what thou wilt, whenever thou wilt: but those riches, that thou mayest never hunger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The health of Thy countenance, O God, hath taken Me up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what purpose?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In order that no longer I may be poor, no longer sorrowful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will praise the name of the Lord with a song, I will magnify Him in praise” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now it hath been said, this poor One praiseth the name of the Lord with a song, he magnifieth Him in praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When would He have ventured to sing, unless He had been refreshed from hunger?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will magnify Him with praise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O vast riches!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What jewels of God’s praise hath he brought out of his inward treasures!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are my riches!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hath given, the Lord hath taken away.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then miserable he hath remained?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See the riches: “As it hath pleased the Lord, so hath been done, be the name of the Lord blessed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And it shall please God:” that I shall praise Him, shall please: “above a new calf, bearing horns and hoofs.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: More grateful to Him shall be the sacrifice of praise than the sacrifice of a calf.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The sacrifice of praise shall glorify me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Immolate to God the sacrifice of praise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then His praise going forth from my mouth shall please God more than a great victim led up to His altar.…Therefore above this calf my praising shall please Thee, such as hereafter will be, after poverty and sorrow, in the eternal society of Angels, where neither adversary there shall be in battle to be tossed, nor sluggard from earth to be stirred up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the needy see and rejoice” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them believe, and in hope be glad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them be more needy, in order that they may deserve to be filled: lest while they belch out pride’s satiety, there be denied them the bread whereon they may healthily live.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Seek the Lord,” ye needy, hunger ye and thirst; for He is Himself the living bread that came down from Heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Seek ye the Lord, and your soul shall live.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye seek bread, that your flesh may live: the Lord seek ye, that your soul may live.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord hath hearkened to the poor” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath hearkened to the poor, and He would not have hearkened to the poor, unless they were poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou be hearkened to?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Poor be thou: let sorrow cry out from thee, and not fastidiousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And His fettered ones He hath not despised.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Being offended at His servants, He hath put them in fetters: but them crying from the fetters He hath not despised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are these fetters?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Mortality, the corruptibleness of the flesh are the fetters wherewith we have been bound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And would ye know the weight of these fetters?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of them is said, “The body which is corrupted weigheth down the soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whenever men in the world will to be rich, for these fetters they are seeking rags.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let the rags of the fetters suffice: seek so much as is necessary for keeping off want, but when thou seekest superfluities, thou longest to load thy fetters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In such a prison then let the fetters abide even alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sufficient for the day be the evil thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let there praise Him heavens and earth, sea and all things creeping in them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The true riches of this poor man are these, to consider the creation, and to praise the Creator.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let there praise Him heavens and earth, sea and all things creeping therein.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And doth this creation alone praise God, when by considering of it God is praised?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear thou another thing also: “for God shall save Sion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He restoreth His Church, the faithful Gentiles He doth incorporate with His Only-Begotten; He beguileth not them that believe in Him of the reward of His promise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For God shall save Sion; and there shall be builded the cities of Juda.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These same are the Churches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one say, when shall it come to pass that there be builded the cities of Juda?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O that thou wouldest acknowledge the Edifice, and be a living stone, that thou mightest enter into Her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even now the cities of Juda are being built.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Juda is interpreted confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By confession of humility there are being builded the cities of Juda: in order that there may remain without the proud, who blush to confess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For God shall save Sion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear in the following words: “and the seed of His servants shall possess Her, and they that love His name shall dwell therein” (ver.
